mnemonics.title: HRS Triad
mnemonics.content: HRS = HttpServletRequest, HttpServletResponse, HttpSession. Remember: 'Handle Requests Smoothly' using these three core components

mnemonics.title: GET-POST-DELETE Chain
mnemonics.content: Order of HTTP methods: GPD (GET, POST, DELETE) → 'Good Pizza Delivery' helps recall common HttpServlet methods: doGet(), doPost(), doDelete()

mnemonics.title: Session Steps
mnemonics.content: SESSION = Store, Expiry, Security, Serve, Identify, Override, New. Remembers key HttpSession methods: setAttribute(), getMaxInactiveInterval(), isNew(), etc.

mnemonics.title: Cookie Lifecycle
mnemonics.content: B.A.K.E.S → Build (new Cookie()), Add (response.addCookie()), Keep (client stores), Extract (request.getCookies()), Secure (setSecure())

acronyms.usage: Core triad classes in jakarta.servlet.http package
Mapped to HTTP methods: POST GET PUT DELETE in HttpServlet
Remembers cookie purpose for session tracking

visualTips.visualization: Imagine an Amazon delivery box with labels (headers), contents (parameters), and return address (client info)
Picture a printer with paper tray (output stream), status LED (status codes), and sticker sheet (headers)
Visualize a locker storing shopping bags (attributes) with a timer (session timeout)
See a relay race baton handoff between servlets

analogies.analogy: Like a restaurant order form - contains what the customer wants (parameters), special instructions (headers), and table number (client info)
Similar to a food delivery - contains the meal (content), receipt (status code), and packaging instructions (headers)
Like a gym locker - stores user's belongings (attributes) temporarily and clears when timer expires
Similar to loyalty cards - small stored preferences that identify returning customers
